name: Prompt → Product Pipeline
desc: How a raw prompt becomes a monetized product. Six layers, each with its own tool.
blueprint:
LAYER 1 (Prompt) → ACTIVATE master prompt + modifiers + animal mode
LAYER 2 (Validate) → VALIDATE lint + scoring + word swaps to harden the prompt
LAYER 3 (Compose) → BUILDER workflow builder chains animals into a system prompt
LAYER 4 (Build) → fullstack-dev skill ships the Next.js app
LAYER 5 (Monetize) → MONETIZE recipes (Stripe + credit system + pricing)
LAYER 6 (Distribute) → SAAS_TEMPLATES + AUTOMATION_WORKFLOWS for go-to-market
connections: ACTIVATE.master → VALIDATE.lint (hardening loop)
BUILDER.workflow → fullstack-dev skill (code generation)
MONETIZE_RECIPES['ai-wrapper-saas'] → DEPLOY_STACKS['vercel-supabase-stripe']
AUTOMATION_WORKFLOWS → n8n / Zapier / Make for distribution
failure_modes: Skipping VALIDATE → prompt leaks PII or hallucinates → production incident
Building before pricing → no monetization hook → free forever
No automation layer → manual ops cost kills margin at scale

name: Animal Chain Architecture
desc: How the 7 animal modes compose into reasoning chains. Each chain addresses a different cognitive bottleneck.
blueprint:
EAGLE → BEAVER → DOLPHIN → ANT (Vision → System → Creative → Steps)
  Use for: greenfield product builds. Eagle sets strategy, Beaver designs the system, Dolphin invents the differentiator, Ant ships the first version.

OWL → ELEPHANT → RABBIT (Analysis → Cross-field → Multiply)
  Use for: research and content. Owl surfaces hidden factors, Elephant connects to other fields, Rabbit multiplies into publishable variations.

BEAVER → OWL → ANT (Build → Audit → Steps)
  Use for: refactors and migrations. Beaver designs the new system, Owl audits risks, Ant sequences the migration steps.

DOLPHIN → EAGLE → BEAVER (Creative → Strategy → System)
  Use for: pivots. Dolphin generates options, Eagle picks the strategic one, Beaver builds the new system.
connections: ANIMALS × CHAINS → 15 named chains in source data
WF (workflows) → each workflow IS a named chain applied to a domain
BUILDER.workflow_builder → user-composable chains
failure_modes: Starting with Ant (steps) before Eagle (strategy) → ships the wrong thing fast
Using only one animal → single-mode thinking, no perspective diversity
Skipping Owl in technical decisions → hidden factors surface in production

name: Design Combo Selector
desc: Decision tree for picking the right design combo from the 20 rated options.
blueprint:
IF audience = consumer/health → 🌱 Warm Minimal (score 9)
IF audience = developer → 🏗 Developer Tool (score 10)
IF audience = executive → 📊 Executive Dash (score 10)
IF audience = creative agency → ⚡ Motion-First (score 9)
IF audience = AI product user → 🔮 AI Product UI (score 10)
IF surface = landing page → 💥 Brutal + Neon OR 🎬 Cinematic
IF surface = dashboard → 📊 Executive Dash OR 📊 Data Dense
IF surface = content → 📰 Editorial + Bento OR 🎭 Editorial Dark
IF surface = marketing/launch → 🚀 Full Immersive (with senior motion designer only)
IF budget < $5k → ✨ Micro + Skeleton (always safe)
IF budget > $50k → 💎 Premium Minimal OR 🎬 Cinematic
connections: COMBOS (20 entries) → COMBO_RATINGS (synergy score + structural reasoning)
VOCAB (52 terms) → individual elements that compose into combos
TYPO (typography pairings) → applies on top of any combo
AESTHETIC_KEYWORDS (16) → swap-in for [specific aesthetic keyword] in templates
failure_modes: Picking combo by aesthetic preference instead of audience → wrong fit
Stacking 3+ high-motion combos → bundle bloat, accessibility failures
Ignoring the synergy score → low-synergy combos fight each other

name: Monetization Decision Tree
desc: Pick the right monetization recipe based on what you have and who pays.
blueprint:
IF you have an MCP tool → mcp-to-product ($29-99/mo)
IF you have an AI API capability → ai-wrapper-saas ($500-5k MRR)
IF you have a consulting practice → consulting-to-product ($2-10k/project)
IF you have an open-source project → open-source-monetize ($500-20k/mo)
IF you have content/audience → MONETIZE_FW['audience-leverage']
IF you have a dev tool → SAAS_TEMPLATES['devtool-saas']
IF you have nothing yet → start with TOP10_PROMPTS['find-your-pricing']
connections: MONETIZE_RECIPES (8) → SAAS_TEMPLATES (10) → DEPLOY_STACKS (9)
AUTOMATION_WORKFLOWS (6) → distribution multiplier on any recipe
TOP10_PROMPTS (15) → prompt-level tools to execute any recipe
failure_modes: Pricing too low → no margin for support/refunds/iter
Pricing too high → no first customers, no social proof
Skipping automation → manual ops cap growth at ~50 customers

name: Motion System Layers
desc: Four motion layers stack — each addresses a different timing need.
blueprint:
LAYER 1 (Micro, 100-200ms) → button hovers, copy flashes, focus rings
  Tool: CSS transitions, framer-motion useSpring
LAYER 2 (Section, 250-400ms) → zone enter, modal open, drawer slide
  Tool: CSS keyframes, framer-motion AnimatePresence
LAYER 3 (Scroll, 600-1500ms) → parallax, pinned sections, scrub
  Tool: GSAP ScrollTrigger (or framer-motion useScroll)
LAYER 4 (Ambient, infinite) → background loops, particle drift, gradient shift
  Tool: CSS @keyframes infinite, Three.js, Lottie
connections: ANIM_LIBS (14 options) → pick one per layer
CONSTRAINT_OPTS → includes 'reduced-motion' and '60fps budget' as hard constraints
COMBOS with 'kinetic' tag → require Layer 3
failure_modes: Stacking two Layer 3 libs (GSAP + framer-motion useScroll) → bundle bloat, conflict
Layer 4 always-on without prefers-reduced-motion → accessibility violation
Layer 1 too slow (>200ms) → app feels sluggish, not premium

name: Skills Health Compounding
desc: How skill stacks compound (or decay) over time. Health is a function of compatibility, maintenance, and bundle cost.
blueprint:
HEALTH = compatibility × maintenance × bundle_discipline

COMPATIBILITY (do they conflict?)
  +1 if both TS-native
  +1 if no overlapping responsibilities
  -1 if competing patterns (e.g. two state libs)
  -2 if version conflicts (React 18 vs 19)

MAINTENANCE (will they break?)
  +1 if backed by a company (21st.dev, GSAP)
  +1 if >1k GitHub stars
  -1 if solo maintainer
  -2 if last commit > 6 months ago

BUNDLE_DISCIPLINE (cost control)
  +1 if tree-shakeable
  -1 if ships global CSS
  -2 if > 100kb gzipped

Compounding: a healthy stack gets FASTER to build with over time. An unhealthy stack gets slower (each new feature fights the others).
connections: SKILL_STACKS (6 curated) → all pre-vetted for compatibility
TOOL_MATRIX (4) → external tool integrations
DEPLOY_STACKS (9) → where the stack actually runs
failure_modes: Adding skills without health check → tech debt compounds invisibly
Lock-in to a single skill author → bus-factor risk
No bundle budget → LCP > 2.5s, users bounce
